KEY DIVERGENCES to flag for the designer: (1) the four .assignee-avatar color variants (.blue/.green/.purple) and the per-person hex gradients in the 44px row collapse to the single tokenized brand-gradient fallback (bg-primary/text-on-primary) because the real Avatar component has no color/variant prop for the legacy palette-step gradients — those gradients are a documented DS gap (vacancy-card.css). (2) The focus-state chip uses the real Chip with the page-local .bd-sim-focus class to force the ring visible (the real component only shows focus-ring on keyboard :focus-visible), matching the original demo intent. (3) Chip has no explicit disabled visual variant in the package, so the disabled demo adds opacity-[0.38] cursor-not-allowed (Embassy disabled convention) alongside the native disabled attribute. InputChip renders its own remove (X) icon, so no manual icon needed for the removable variants.
